Title: GS Gill Sports, long-time Adidas retailer, to shut landmark outlet
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: GS Gill Sports, a retailer that predates Malaysia's independence, is set to close its outlet on Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman and move its operations online.
The iconic store at Wisma Harwant, one of Kuala Lumpur's best-known shopping landmarks, has been a fixture of the city's retail scene for decades.
News of the closure spread across social media this week after posts on Facebook and Threads announced a clearance sale until June 30 before the store pulls its shutters for the final time.
The posts said the company is not ceasing operations and will instead transition its business online.
For many Malaysians, the announcement sparked a wave of nostalgia.
"This is where I bought my original Adidas shoes around 1979," one user wrote.
Another recalled purchasing France's 1998 World Cup jersey from the store for RM80, adding that it remains wearable today.
Others remembered GS Gill as a trusted destination for authentic Adidas products long before online shopping and international sports retailers became commonplace.
One Threads user said sports enthusiasts from the pre-e-commerce era would undoubtedly remember GS Gill, describing it as the company that first introduced Adidas to Malaysia.
Founded by Tan Sri GS Gill, who died aged 92 on Aug 27, 2016, the business began as a small shop on Batu Road, now Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman, in 1946.
It later expanded into the 11-storey Wisma Harwant, named after his wife.
In 1952, GS Gill became the licensor of Adidas, then an emerging brand in Europe.
The New Straits Times has contacted the company for further details on the closure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Civil service pension revamp crucial to protect nation's fiscal health, say economists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government's proposal for a new retirement scheme for civil servants is a necessary structural reform, economists said.
They said the reform is crucial to prevent soaring civil service pension liabilities from crippling the nation's fiscal health.
Sunway University economics professor Dr Yeah Kim Leng said the current pension framework is no longer sustainable on its current trajectory, noting that the projected RM46.36 billion pension bill by 2030 would consume too much of the country's revenue.
"The pension liability will balloon to a level that will likely constitute a major portion of operational expenditure.
RM46 billion is almost equivalent to two to three per cent of Gross Domestic Product (GDP).
"This means every year, the government has to set aside two to three per cent of GDP just to pay for services already rendered in the past.
"This classic trade-off creates a negative fiscal impact, leaving less money for other productive public investments," he told the New Straits Times.
He was responding to Prime Minister Datuk Seri
Anwar Ibrahim
, who said the government is fine-tuning a new retirement mechanism to ensure long-term fiscal stability while continuing to attract top talent.
Anwar said the proposed mechanism for the civil service retirement scheme, known as the "contributory permanent appointment" scheme, is still being studied.
Yeah said shifting to an Employees Provident Fund (EPF)-like contribution system, where both the government and civil servants make regular percentage contributions, is the right move, but it has to be introduced gradually.
On whether the abolition of traditional pensions would deter top talent from joining the civil service, Yeah said the new scheme could be designed to mirror the financial security of the old system.
"Some employees prefer the traditional scheme for its security. However, the new system can be structured using an EPF-style model that incorporates annuity-like features, allowing retirees to withdraw a regular, fixed monthly income stream rather than just a lump sum.
"Under this model, the private and public sectors will converge. It becomes neutral whether you work for the government or the private sector, as both pathways will guarantee minimum income security upon retirement," he said.
He said that a contributory system would grant civil servants greater career mobility, making it much easier for talent to migrate between the public and private sectors without forfeiting their accumulated retirement savings.
He said that currently, those who resign before the minimum required period for a pension lose everything, whereas a portable retirement fund provides an attractive pathway and supports long-term plans to downsize the civil service structure.
He added that while recruits will immediately transition to the new framework, effectively halting the growth of future liabilities, the government must still brace for the immediate financial burden of existing civil servants.
"For existing pensioners and current civil servants on the old scheme, the government has no choice but to continue honouring those liabilities.
"This means increasing allocations for the current pension fund so it can grow at a rate capable of meeting future withdrawals. It is unavoidable, but stopping the influx of new liabilities means the system will eventually reach a sustainable equilibrium."
Another economist, former Penang Institute chief executive officer and head of economics Dr Lim Kim-Hwa, said the civil service retirement scheme revamp is necessary to replace an unsustainable system and bring Malaysia in line with global practices.
He said doing nothing would hurt future generations by forcing them to carry an unsustainable financial burden.
Lim said the current system is too expensive because civil servants are living longer, which naturally drives up pension costs.
He  said stopping traditional pensions will not hurt recruitment, noting that the private sector successfully hires top talent without them.
With similar pension systems closing worldwide, Lim said it is natural for the government to modernise its retirement packages.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Diesel subsidies extended to small traders with company vehicles
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Small traders with company-registered jeeps and pickup trucks will soon enjoy diesel subsidies.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said these were among the improvements to the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) that were discussed by the cabinet yesterday.
"Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim directed that several improvements be made to ensure public feedback is taken into account through strategies and approaches that consider the cost of living, the management of subsidy leakages, and the country's fiscal position and capacity," Armizan said in a statement.
On the expansion of diesel subsidies, Armizan said they would apply to small traders with company-registered vehicles classified under the "business private use" category.
Previously, diesel subsidy eligibility was limited to vehicles registered under an individual's name through the BUDI Diesel programme and company-registered goods transport vehicles.
Armizan said the government will also review and adjust the fixed diesel quota for several categories of commercial land transport vehicles under SKDS.
The revised quota will go beyond assessing average fuel consumption by vehicle type, and take into account other technical factors.
These factors include gross vehicle weight, engine capacity, lorry category, fuel consumption levels, and vehicle operating patterns.
"Under SKDS, fuel usage rates and the amount of government fuel subsidy received by each registered vehicle and company will be recorded," Armizan said.
He added that his ministry, as well as the finance and transport ministries, have been tasked with immediate follow-up action, including identifying suitable subsidy delivery mechanisms.
Separately, he said all 4,146 petrol stations nationwide were ready for the full rollout of BUDI Diesel on July 1, which will see subsidised diesel sold at RM2.10 per litre.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jalaluddin: Umno proposed PKR, Amanah leaders to replace Aminuddin as MB
Article:
JELEBU: Negri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias today claimed Barisan Nasional (BN) had proposed two Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders to replace Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as menteri besar after the party withdrew its support for him during the state's political crisis.
Jalaluddin said the proposed candidates were Pilah assemblyman Datuk Noorzunita Begum Mohd Ibrahim, who is also a PKR state executive councillor, and Klawang assemblyman Datuk Bakri Sawir of Amanah.
The BN Negri Sembilan deputy chairman said he proposed the two names to what he described as a "key figure in Putrajaya", who contacted him after Umno withdrew its support for Aminuddin on April 27.
"Today I want to tell the public. During the crisis after we withdrew our support, I was contacted and visited by an important figure from Putrajaya who asked us to preserve the stability of the state government and allow it to continue until the end of its term.
"I said, 'Alright, no problem.' I suggested appointing a menteri besar from within PH. Datuk Noorzunita was one option, and former engineer Datuk Bakri was also qualified to be appointed menteri besar.
"However, the proposal was not implemented," he said after officiating the Jelebu Umno division conference today.
Jalaluddin was responding to allegations that he had sought the menteri besar's post by leading 13 Umno assemblymen to withdraw support for Aminuddin.
He said that if he had wanted the position, he could have pursued it immediately after the 2023 state election instead of waiting more than two years.
"After the state election, BN won 14 seats in the State Legislative Assembly, ahead of DAP with 11, PKR with five, Amanah with one and Perikatan Nasional with five.
"Grassroots members kept asking, 'What are we waiting for, Datuk Seri?' They believed we deserved the post because we had secured a bigger mandate from the people.
"Why didn't I follow their wishes? Because we wanted to respect the mandate and trust placed by the federal leadership, namely Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also PH chairman," he said.
Jalaluddin also rejected claims that Umno was responsible for the early dissolution of the State Legislative Assembly before the end of its term.
He said it was Aminuddin who sought the dissolution from the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, not Umno.
"I was no longer a member of the state executive council at that time. Yet Umno was blamed."
He urged the public to examine the sequence of events carefully before drawing conclusions about the political crisis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt to review fixed quota for SKDS subsidised diesel
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government will review the fixed quota rates of subsidised diesel for several types of land transport vehicles for goods under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) to ensure that the subsidy distribution is more accurate and in line with actual operational needs.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the decision was made following yesterday's Cabinet Meeting which discussed the status of progress in implementing targeted fuel subsidy through the Budi Diesel, BUDI95, Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) mechanisms.
"Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ordered improvements to be implemented in several aspects to ensure that the people's feedback is met through strategies and approaches that take into account the people's cost of living factors, leakage risk management and the country's position and fiscal space," he said in a press conference after a field session in conjunction with the implementation of the Budi Diesel early access, here today.
Armizan said under the improvements, which are expected to be implemented by August, the adjustment of diesel quotas under SKDS will no longer be based solely on average consumption by vehicle type, but will also take into account several technical factors.
Among the factors that will be assessed are the weight with load (BDM), engine capacity, lorry category, fuel consumption levels and the operating pattern of each vehicle.
"There are vehicles of the same type of transport but different BDMs, so if previously there was a uniform fixed quota for the type of transport, which was the same, whereas in terms of usage we know that transport vehicles with larger BDMs in terms of weight, size and also the size of the load, then the fuel consumption would definitely be more.
"So yesterday the Cabinet decided to make an adjustment to the fixed quota not only by looking at the average previous usage, we have the previous usage record, but also by looking at other technical factors so that the quota is more in line with the operating costs of companies involved in the transportation of consumer goods," he said.
He said the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, the Transport Ministry (MOT) and the Finance Ministry (MOF) had been instructed to take immediate follow-up action to implement the Cabinet's policy decisions by identifying appropriate channeling mechanisms including the need for modifications and integration into the existing implementation system.
"God willing, KPDN, MOT and MOF will work closely so that the policy decisions made by the Cabinet yesterday is implemented immediately so that more people would benefit from the targeted fuel subsidy agenda," he said.
In addition, Armizan said the Cabinet also agreed to expand the distribution of diesel subsidies to small traders who have diesel vehicles such as jeeps and pickups registered in the name of a business or company under the class or code of private business use.
He said previously, the eligibility conditions for diesel subsidies were limited to vehicles registered in the name of individuals for Budi Diesel and business vehicles or goods transport companies under the class or code of use for consumer goods transport for SKDS.
"Yesterday the Cabinet decided to expand this policy because it saw that these were small businesses that we needed to support," he said.
Meanwhile, Armizan said owners of private diesel vehicles such as jeeps and pickups who were eligible under Budi Diesel and wanted to apply for an additional 100-litre quota, bringing the total quota to 300 litres compared to the basic quota of 200 litres, could apply online or fill out a physical form.
"There are still those who think that the quota for BUDI diesel is only 200 litres, which is actually not correct, the basic quota is 200 litres for all those who have diesel vehicles registered in the name of individuals, but those who have diesel vehicles such as jeeps and pickups can apply for an additional 100 litres, so it is actually 300 litres.
"Actually, the way to apply is easy, it is on the official Budi Madani portal at www.budimadani.gov.my.
Anyone who wants to apply physically, can go to the nearest Inland Revenue Board (IRB) office, there will be an officer there who will assist, he said.
Armizan said IRB offices in Sabah and Sarawak will be open tomorrow for the purpose of applying for additional Budi Diesel quotas.
Commenting on the results of a field trip session at a petrol station here following the implementation of the early access Budi Diesel for private diesel vehicles in Peninsular Malaysia, Armizan said he was satisfied with the level of readiness of the stations involved.
During the early access period, eligible consumers can buy subsidised diesel at RM2.15 per litre through MyKad verification at participating petrol stations.
"The survey covered the level of implementation preparation at the retail level of petrol stations including the stability of the transaction system, the verification process using MyKad and the oil company's digital application, training for staff as well as aspects of customer-friendly services to ensure the smooth operation of the mechanism once it comes into effect," he said.
He said that as a result of close collaboration between the government and the industry, 10 oil companies, namely Petronas, Shell, Petron, Caltex, BHPetrol, Five, Smart Stream, Xcel, PETROS and IPTB, had confirmed their respective readiness for the implementation of Budi Diesel.
A total of 4,146 petrol stations nationwide were ready for the full implementation of Budi Diesel as one of the fuel subsidy targeting mechanisms.
Through Budi Diesel, eligible Malaysians will enjoy subsidized diesel at RM2.10 per litre at petrol stations either through MyKad verification or the company's digital application.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Faris Johari installed as new Undang of Sungei Ujong, Hassan as Undang of Rembau
Article:
KUALA PILAH: The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today formalised the installation of two new Undang (ruling chieftains) in separate ceremonies at Istana Besar Seri Menanti, underscoring the continued importance of adat and consensus in the state's governance structure.
In the morning ceremony, Muhammad Faris Johari, 28, was installed as the 11th Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong, bearing the title Datuk Klana Petra, while in the afternoon, Hassan Ab Hamid, 67, was installed as the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau, with the title Datuk Sedia Raja.
The morning ceremony began with the Orang Empat Istana seeking royal consent to commence proceedings, before the newly appointed Sungei Ujong Undang paid homage and pledged allegiance to the Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
In his royal address, Tuanku Muhriz expressed hope that the pledges made would always be upheld with sincerity, integrity and a strong sense of responsibility.
"I remind my Orang Kaya (chieftains) to remain loyal to the institution of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan and to maintain a close and respectful relationship with me and the Palace," the ruler said during the installation ceremony.
Also in attendance were Tunku Ampuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud, Tunku Besar Seri Menanti Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, and Tunku Panglima Besar Tunku Zain Al-'Abidin.
The ceremony was also witnessed by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and heads of state government departments.
Tuanku Muhriz said relationships founded on mutual respect, trust and consensus were essential to preserving the constitutional monarchy, safeguarding customary traditions and ensuring the state's continued well-being.
He also called on the people to work closely with the customary leadership in carrying out their responsibilities while continuing to uphold and preserve the values of Adat Perpatih practised in the state and passed down through generations.
Born on Oct 30, 1997, Faris holds a bachelor's degree in accountancy from Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) and obtained his professional qualification through the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) programme in 2022.
He is married to Sharifah Amirah Syed Ismail Ash-Shahab.
The afternoon ceremony continued with the installation of Hassan as the new Undang of Luak Rembau.
Earlier, Hassan and his wife, Asmah Yaakob, were escorted to the palace in a procession of about 300 people, accompanied by kompang percussion, before the Orang Empat Istana presented ceremonial offerings to seek royal consent to begin the ceremony.
The new Rembau Undang then paid homage and pledged allegiance to the ruler.
In his royal address, Tuanku Muhriz described the appointment as a significant trust arising from adat consensus, which must be carried out with sincerity, wisdom and responsibility to safeguard tradition and ensure the welfare of the people in Rembau.
He said any disputes should be managed with wisdom, diplomacy and a spirit of consensus.
Hassan, born on Jan 11, 1959, in Rembau and from the Biduanda Sedia Raja clan of Kampung Tanjung, is a retired teacher. He began his career in 1984 and later served in several schools before retiring in 2017 as Senior Assistant for Student Affairs at SK Astana Raja.
The ceremonies reflect the enduring role of traditional institutions in Negeri Sembilan's governance system, which blends monarchy, adat and community consensus.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesian man walks across 17 countries to fulfil dream of praying in the Holy Land
Article:
PASIR MAS: Armed with faith, determination and a backpack, an Indonesian man walked across 17 countries to fulfil his lifelong dream of praying in the Holy Land.
Vico Nurfaisal, 40, the youngest of five siblings, arrived in Malaysia via Sungai Golok on Thursday and is now making his way south through Kelantan, Terengganu and Johor before continuing his overland journey home to Indonesia.
Vico said his expedition began with a single purpose — to perform prayers at Masjidil Haram in Mecca and Masjid Nabawi in Madinah through a journey driven by faith and perseverance.
"I reached Mecca on Jan 26 after walking through 17 countries.
"I had no sponsors. I relied only on God throughout the journey," he said when met here yesterday.
He said travelling on foot across multiple countries required him to spend much of his savings on daily necessities.
Despite completing the arduous journey to Mecca, Vico said the money he had left was only enough to buy a flight ticket out of Saudi Arabia.
"I could not continue walking from Saudi Arabia because several countries along the route are experiencing conflict, making the journey far too dangerous.
"So I flew out of Saudi Arabia on June 1 and resumed walking after arriving in Thailand because I no longer had enough money to continue travelling by air," he said.
Vico crossed into Malaysia from Thailand and is currently in Pasir Mas before heading to Kota Bharu and continuing his journey south.
He hopes to reach Indonesia in about two months if everything goes according to plan.
Although he has not kept track of the total distance covered, one leg of the journey remains especially memorable.
After arriving in Mecca, he walked another 518km to Madinah over eight days to perform prayers at Masjid Nabawi.
"For me, this expedition was about reaching the two holy mosques through my own effort.
"There is still one mosque left — Al-Aqsa Mosque in Palestine.
"That will be my second expedition one day, walking from Indonesia to Palestine, God willing," he said.
For Vico, the journey is not about setting records or seeking recognition, but about proving that faith, patience and determination can carry a person farther than they ever imagined.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-security guard gets 12 years' jail, rotan for raping Australian tourist
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A former hotel security guard was sentenced to 12 years' jail and 12 strokes of the rotan today for raping and sodomising an Australian tourist last year.
Sessions Court judge Juraidah Abbas meted out the sentence after S. Prasanth, 25, pleaded guilty to both charges before the trial commenced.
For the first charge of rape under Section 376(2)(b) of the Penal Code, Prasanth was sentenced to 12 years' jail and eight strokes of the rotan.
For the second charge of carnal intercourse against the order of nature under Section 377C of the Penal Code, he received 10 years' imprisonment and four strokes of the rotan.
Juraidah ordered both sentences to run concurrently from the date of arrest.
Deputy Public Prosecutor Anuarul Hakim Abdul Muen, in pushing for a deterrent sentence, said rape is a serious and heinous offence.
He said that as the victim is an Australian, crimes of this nature could severely damage Malaysia's international reputation and its bilateral relations with Australia.
"The prosecution urges the court to impose a deterrent and appropriate sentence in the public interest," Anuarul Hakim said.
In mitigation, defence counsel Arvind Santhanam said his client had shown genuine remorse by pleading guilty prior to the trial.
Arvind added that his client accepted full responsibility for his actions and was prepared to face the legal consequences.
According to the facts of the case, Prasanth committed the offences inside a Perodua Axia at the Hong Seng Estate Chinese cemetery between 5.20am and 6pm on June 28 last year.
He had placed the victim in fear of death or injury by pointing a pair of scissors at her.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Gazetting Bandar Malaysia land as Malay Reserve requires courage, consensus
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today described the decision to gazette 50 acres (20.23ha) of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land as a significant move to safeguard Bumiputera interests, saying it required both political courage and careful consensus-building.
He said the government reclaimed the 480-acre Bandar Malaysia site after the project stalled due to alleged abuses under the previous administration. The land is now managed by Khazanah Nasional Bhd and Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas).
"The 50 acres is equivalent in value to 5,000 acres in Kedah. It is not insignificant, and I know this will be controversial if it is not handled properly.
"I emphasise that this decision is to safeguard Bumiputera interests, which is very important and critical to me. It requires considerable political courage and wisdom in engaging with all sectors," he said at the opening of the National Convention of the Syariah Legal Profession 2026 here today.
Anwar stressed that the move does not involve taking land from any community, as the area is already government-owned.
He said the government had also briefed leaders from various communities on the rationale behind the decision to prevent misunderstandings and ensure its smooth implementation.
On June 27 last year, the Finance Ministry said further studies were being conducted on the development strategy for the 50-acre Malay Reserve Land within Bandar Malaysia to ensure it aligns with the government's aspiration of transforming the project into an international business hub.
The ministry said several key principles were being considered, including clustered residential areas near facilities such as mosques, integrated development in line with the inclusivity principles of Malaysia MADANI, and the creation of cultural tourism attractions showcasing Malay heritage.
The project, located on the former Kuala Lumpur Air Base in Sungai Besi, is expected to have a gross development value of RM140 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Budi Diesel rollout smooth as early access phase begins
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Eligible Malaysians began enjoying fuel cost savings today as the early access phase of the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) programme commenced, with the purchase of subsidised diesel at petrol stations across Peninsular Malaysia proceeding smoothly.
Under the early access phase in Peninsular Malaysia, subsidised diesel is sold at RM2.15 per litre using MyKad verification at participating petrol stations. The programme will be fully rolled out nationwide on July 1, with eligible Malaysians able to purchase subsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre.
A check by Bernama at a petrol station along Jalan Tun Razak here found that the purchase of subsidised diesel using MyKad verification proceeded smoothly without any issues.
A motorist, Hazwan Nik Ismail, 28, said the process of purchasing diesel using MyKad was straightforward and took only a short time.
"The application process to obtain the diesel subsidy is also simple, as it can be completed online," he said.
In
KEDAH
, the supervisor of a petrol station in Seberang Pendang, Mohd Faizal Ishak, said the station conducted two system tests yesterday and today to ensure transactions proceeded smoothly.
He said that as of 11am, nearly 10 eligible customers had successfully purchased subsidised diesel without any disruptions.
Meanwhile, durian orchard operator Ai Tean Ai Ching, 65, said the targeted Budi Diesel subsidy had greatly helped Malaysians cope with the rising cost of living.
"I only work at my orchard. Today, I purchased RM70 worth of diesel and received 32.56 litres. Without the subsidy, I would have had to pay RM132.51. This assistance has truly eased my financial burden," he said.
In
PERAK
, small-scale food trader Mohammad Fairuz Ramli, 46, said his four-wheel-drive vehicle was essential for collecting supplies and making deliveries, with his fuel expenses reaching nearly RM1,000 a week.
"With the government's introduction of this initiative, I can save around 30 to 40 per cent on my diesel expenses. The savings can be used to cover other operating costs of my business," he said.
In SELANGOR, logistics company owner Muhammad Khoidin, 42, said his business had been given a new lease of life following the implementation of Budi Diesel, which has helped ease the financial burden caused by higher diesel prices.
"My small company's operations were at risk of being severely affected. But now I can breathe a sigh of relief as the government has implemented the targeted subsidy mechanism," he said.
Meanwhile, in PAHANG, Marilyn Shi Ming, 42, said she had rarely used her four-wheel-drive vehicle because of the high cost of diesel.
"Previously, I would only start the engine and drive it for a short distance. Now I can take it on longer trips," she said.
However, she hopes the government will reconsider the Budi Diesel quota mechanism, which is currently shared with the Budi RON95 quota.
In KELANTAN, Mohd Asri Musa, 60, said the implementation of Budi Diesel had brought relief to diesel vehicle owners like himself who had been affected by the rise in diesel prices.
Meanwhile, Pertubuhan Mesra Pengguna Malaysia chairman Datuk Seri Haniff Omar said the targeted subsidy mechanism under the BUDI Diesel programme was fairer and ensured that assistance reached those who genuinely qualified.
"The price difference between subsidised and unsubsidised diesel is quite significant. This assistance can help reduce the burden on users who have no choice but to rely on diesel-powered vehicles every day," he said.
-- Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World's first anti-bullying tribunal: Madani govt's stand against bully culture
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Malaysia has made history by becoming the first nation in the world to set up an Anti-Bullying Tribunal, a dedicated and effective mechanism to tackle bullying head-on.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the tribunal's creation reflects the Madani government's resolve to ensure that bullying never takes root as a cultural norm in society.
"No other country has such a tribunal. There are sexual harassment tribunals, but an Anti-Bullying Tribunal has never been established before.
"The Madani government is fully aware that we cannot allow a culture of bullying to spread unchecked.
"That is precisely why we are introducing this tribunal, as a more fitting approach to handle cases involving children and teenagers," she told Bernama after the Johor Anti-Bullying Literacy programme at the Westin Desaru Convention Centre here today.
Azalina, who is also the Pengerang member of parliament, said the quasi-judicial body is designed to overcome systemic bottlenecks in handling civil bullying cases through regular courts, where some cases can drag on for up to nine years.
She said the Anti-Bullying Act 2026 is rooted in a restorative approach, one that not only shields and heals victims but also looks at the circumstances surrounding the perpetrators, who may themselves be wrestling with difficult environments.
"Sometimes both sides come with their own baggage, family issues, personal struggles. We want to strike a fair balance, without shying away from firm action against bullying," she said.
On cyberbullying, Azalina said the tribunal will assess every case brought before it, including those involving online content, strictly on its facts and in line with the law.
The Anti-Bullying Act 2026 and the Anti-Bullying Tribunal officially took effect on June 16, and currently apply to individuals aged 18 and below, she added.
However, she said the government will keep the door open to expanding its scope in future, should the need arise.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM to join Johor campaign trail, PH grand finale
Article:
KULAI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to hit the campaign trail in Johor, with a grand finale Pakatan Harapan (PH) programme scheduled for July 9, just two days before Johoreans head to the polls.
PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail confirmed the news, saying the prime minister will be lending his weight to the PH campaign.
"Yes, the PM will be coming down," he said after a walkabout with PH candidates contesting seven state seats.
The candidates were Arthur Chiong Sen Sern (Bukit Batu), Mohd Syafwan Ani (Bukit Permai), Wong Bor Yang (Senai), Yeo Tung Siong (Pekan Nanas), Haniff @ Ghazali Hosman (Pulai Sebatang), Guna Balakrishnan (Layang-Layang) and Nor Hafiz Roslan (Machap).
"It depends on how happy he is with our performance. If he's satisfied with me, Amir (PKR vice-president), and the machinery, he might not come often. But if he's here frequently, that means we have problems," Saifuddin joked when pressed on how often Anwar would show up.
The PH grand finale on July 9 will also see all component party presidents in attendance, Saifuddin revealed.
"We'll announce more details later. All the presidents will be there," he said, adding that the Prime Minister's schedule would be confirmed closer to the date.
"For now, our focus is on the campaign," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 11-year-old killed after motorcycle being towed is hit by car
Article:
JERTIH: An 11-year-old boy was killed after the motorcycle he was riding, which had broken down and was being towed by another motorcycle, was hit by a car along Jalan Kota Bharu-Kuala Terengganu near Kampung Bukit Kenak here late last night.
In the 11.30pm incident, Wan Julaini Wan Noh, from Kampung Pulau Panjang, died at the scene from severe head and bodily injuries.
Besut police chief Superintendent Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahman said the accident involved a Perodua Axia and a Modenas Dinamik motorcycle.
"Initial investigations found that the victim's motorcycle had broken down and was being towed by another motorcycle at the time of the incident.
"A Perodua Axia travelling in the same direction struck the rear of the victim's motorcycle, while the motorcycle towing it was not involved in the collision," he said in a statement today.
He said the impact dragged the victim for about 50m, causing fatal injuries.
The victim's body was taken to Besut Hospital for a post-mortem.
The 21-year-old female driver of the Perodua Axia was unhurt.
Rozaime said the driver's urine test returned negative for drugs and the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
He also reminded parents and guardians not to allow minors to ride motorcycles on public roads, saying it was an offence under the law and could lead to fatal accidents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mother dies shielding daughter in Venezuela quake
Article:
CARACAS: A Venezuelan mother has died after saving her daughter during twin earthquakes that struck the country, according to footballer Héctor Bello and local media.
Bello said on Instagram that his wife, named by Venezuelan outlets as Andrea, died protecting their toddler during the quakes.
Rescuers continue searching through rubble after the earthquakes near Caracas killed at least 920 people and injured more than 3,000.
Bello said he travelled to Caracas, where his daughter and the child's aunt were receiving treatment in hospital.
"My daughter and her aunt are doing well, they won't be discharged today — they're staying at the hospital. Thank you so much for supporting me through this immense pain," he said in a post, according to the BBC.
In another post, he wrote: "I'll tell her the story of how you saved her, my love - how you gave your own life for our daughter, how you were a brave woman who never abandoned her, even as you took your last breaths," he said.
In a separate Instagram story, he wrote: "How do I explain to your daughter that you lost your life to save hers, and that I wasn't there to do anything? How do I explain it? Give me strength now."
A local football press organisation, Cumaná de Campeones, said Bello's partner was found dead in the rubble, while their daughter survived the collapse of their home.
US Spanish-language outlet Univision also reported the death.
More fatalities continue to be reported as victims are identified, while many remain missing after the disaster.
Two footballers were among those killed, according to their clubs and the Venezuelan Football Federation.
Caracas Fútbol Club said its under-18 player Razan Sijaa died alongside family members in La Guaira.
It said his "joy, dedication, and camaraderie" would be remembered within the club.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A sanctuary I call home: A journalist's tribute to Malaysia's global standing
Article:
LETTERS:
​Malaysia has been my home for over two decades—a land that welcomed me, shaped my professional journey, and embraced me as one of its own.
Today, as I see Malaysia rise to the 12th spot in the Global Peace Index 2026, I am filled with a profound sense of pride that is both personal and professional.
​As a journalist, I have spent years observing the pulse of this nation.
I have witnessed its resilience, its deep-rooted cultural harmony, and its unwavering commitment to stability.
[[nid:1474265]]
This milestone on the global stage is a reflection of a vision that prioritizes the safety and dignity of every person who walks these streets.
​I wish to offer my deepest, most sincere tribute to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, and Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.
Your visionary leadership has been the guiding force behind this achievement, ensuring that our nation remains a bastion of order and tranquility.
​I also extend my profound gratitude to the Royal Malaysia Police and all our security agencies.
Your tireless vigilance, often conducted behind the scenes, creates the very environment in which our dreams can flourish. You are the steadfast guardians of our daily peace, and this international recognition is a well-deserved testament to your professional excellence and unwavering sense of duty.
​For me, this ranking is deeply significant.
It is a validation of the country I chose to make my home. My love for Malaysia is not a fleeting sentiment; it is an unshakable bond forged through twenty years of living among a people who prize unity, kindness, and mutual respect.
To report on a nation that is now recognised as one of the safest in the world is, for me, a distinct honor.
​Thank you, Malaysia, for your warmth, your peace, and your stability.
You have been a guiding light in my life, and I am proud to share this news with the world—not only as a journalist, but as someone who loves this country with all my heart. May this peace continue to flourish, and may our nation remain an enduring beacon of hope for all.
JAVAID UR REHMAN
Bureau Chief to Malaysia
92 News Pakistan
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Full list of candidates for 56 seats
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The campaigning for the 16th Johor state election officially kicks off today after the nomination process for the polls concluded this morning.
A total of 172 candidates will contest the 56 seats in the Johor election, with 42 constituencies set to witness multi-cornered contests.
Three seats will see five-cornered contests, while 12 will feature four-cornered battles and 27 will have three-cornered fights. The remaining 14 seats will see straight fights.
Among the key battlegrounds is Machap, where caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi is defending the seat for a second term. The Johor Barisan Nasional chairman is in a straight fight with Pakatan Harapan's Nur Hafiz Roslan.
This election also sees two former ministers in the fray. Former health minister Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba of BN is in a three-cornered race in Pasir Raja.
Meanwhile, former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik of PH will face four opponents in the Puteri Wangsa seat.

Title: Johor polls: PH to fine-tune strategies for 14 straight fights
Article:
KULAI: Pakatan Harapan (PH) will fine-tune its campaign strategies in 14 state seats that are headed for straight fights in the 16th Johor election.
PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, who is also PKR's joint election director, said each seat would be analysed in detail as electoral trends in the country depend heavily on local factors.
He noted that Perikatan Nasional's (PN) decision not to field candidates in all seats had contributed to the straight-fight scenarios.
"We cannot assume that a straight fight automatically gives an advantage to any side.
"Elections in this country are highly constituency-based. We need to look at voter composition, local issues, the strength of the candidates, and their ability to solve issues for residents in their respective areas.
"All these factors will be analysed before we determine the most suitable strategy," he said at a press conference here today.
Among the seats set for a straight fight is Machap, where incumbent and caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi of Barisan Nasional (BN) will face PH's Nor Hafiz Roslan.
Saifuddin said one of the key questions being watched is whether PN voters will shift their support to BN in areas where the coalition is not contesting.
"No one can guarantee vote transferability. There is no certainty on that matter, which is why we must conduct a comprehensive analysis.
"While we have a general strategy deployed across all areas, we also tailor specific strategies according to the conditions and competition patterns of each state seat," he said.
He said PH would not rely on opponents' weaknesses, but would instead focus on strengthening its own machinery, candidates and campaign narrative throughout the period.
The strength of PH stems from its own hard work and preparation, not from the flaws or problems of rival coalitions, he said.
Earlier, Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun announced that
172 candidates
were confirmed eligible to contest the 56 state seats in the Johor election after the nomination process closed at 10am today.
Aside from the 14 straight fights, 27 state seats will see three-cornered battles, 12 seats will involve four-cornered contests, and three seats are set for five-cornered fights.
The EC has fixed July 7 for early voting and July 11 as polling day.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Body in suitcase: Australian tourist held over Thai teen's murder
Article:
BANGKOK: An Australian has been arrested over the suspected murder of a 17-year-old Thai girl whose naked body was found stuffed inside a suitcase abandoned beside railway tracks in Pattaya.
The 46-year-old man from Ballarat, Victoria, was detained by Thai police on Friday evening while allegedly attempting to board a flight at Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi International Airport.
The victim, identified as Tunchanok Donhomla, was reported missing by friends earlier that day before police made the grisly discovery of her body hours later.
According to Thai media, investigators believe the teenager was picked up by the Australian from Pattaya's Beach Road in the early hours of Thursday before the pair travelled to the hotel where he was staying.
CCTV footage released by local media allegedly showed the pair holding hands as they entered the hotel and took a lift to the man's room about 3.30am.
Police said no one else was seen entering the room after them.
"There were signs of a struggle inside," Pattaya city police Superintendent Colonel Anek Sarathongyu was quoted as saying by Viral Press.
Australian media reported that surveillance footage later allegedly captured the Australian leaving the hotel alone while wheeling a large black suitcase.
"Additional footage reportedly showed him riding away on a motorcycle with the luggage strapped to the back," the report said, adding that the victim's friends reported her missing about 5pm on Friday.
It said police arrested the Australian at Bangkok's main international airport about two hours later before escorting him back to Pattaya for questioning.
"Later that night, investigators found the girl's body folded inside a 26-inch black suitcase abandoned beside railway tracks, about 10 minutes from the hotel."
Police said the victim was found naked and had suffered severe facial injuries.
Authorities said the physical characteristics and tattoos matched those of the teenager seen entering the hotel with the suspect.
"The victim entered the hotel room with the suspect but was never seen leaving," Sarathongyu said.
"Later, the Australian was seen leaving with a suitcase. The victim was found dead inside that same suitcase."
During questioning, the Australian reportedly denied killing the teenager.
When asked by reporters why he had allegedly killed the girl, he replied: "I don't know."
Asked about scratches on his neck and body, the man claimed they were caused by spiders.
However, investigators said the injuries appeared consistent with defensive wounds sustained during a struggle.
Police initially charged the Australian with taking a minor for indecent purposes and are continuing to question him on suspicion of murder.
"He has scratch marks across his body consistent with a struggle, but he denies killing her," Sarathongyu said.
"The suspect claims she disappeared from the room while he was asleep and that he knows nothing about what happened."
Local media reported that the man has pleaded not guilty and has yet to be formally charged with murder.
Under Thai law, a murder conviction carries penalties ranging from life imprisonment to the death sentence.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: State polls: Gobind warns against AI misuse, defends DAP's multiracial candidate lineup
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The use of emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI) in campaign materials for the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections must be carefully monitored to prevent confusion and misinformation that could undermine public confidence and security.
Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said he had repeatedly reminded political parties to exercise caution when using AI, stressing that all campaign materials must be authentic and based on accurate information.
He said election campaigning had evolved significantly with advances in technology, presenting new challenges for political parties.
"I have said many times that when we use new technology, it is important to ensure that the information is authentic.
"We must make security a priority. Campaigning today is very different from the past because of new technology, but it is a challenge we must face.
"We in DAP have made the necessary preparations. Whatever materials we use during this election campaign must be authentic and based on accurate information so voters can make informed decisions," he said after launching Pakatan Harapan's (PH) Skudai campaign operations at Taman Ungku Tun Aminah here today.
Also present were Deputy Finance Minister and DAP strategic director Liew Chin Tong, and Deputy Communications Minister and Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching.
Earlier, Gobind, who is also DAP national chairman, accompanied PH candidate J. Kartiyaini (N48 Skudai) to submit her nomination papers at the Iskandar Puteri City Council (MBIP) Hall.
Commenting on the nomination process, he expressed satisfaction that all 17 DAP candidates representing PH had qualified to contest in the Johor election.
The Damansara member of parliament said the focus would now shift to the two-week campaign period, with candidates expected to engage voters and earn their confidence.
"The nomination process for all DAP and PH candidates went smoothly. Now begins the campaign period for the next two weeks.
"It is crucial that every candidate goes to the ground, meets voters and earns their confidence," he said.
Gobind also expressed confidence in DAP's line-up, saying the party had fielded candidates from a broad cross-section of society.
"We have representatives from all backgrounds, including Malay, Chinese and Indian candidates, as well as both men and women.
"This dynamic combination will strengthen Johor DAP because we focus on inclusivity. We want our elected representatives to understand the concerns of every community and help solve the people's problems," he said.
Gobind also dismissed criticism that DAP practises a "one-term, one-race" approach by replacing state assembly candidates after a single term, citing the Skudai seat as an example.
He reiterated that DAP is a multiracial party that champions all communities in Malaysia.
"This is democracy, and we field candidates whom we believe are best suited to represent a particular constituency. We are confident they can serve the community in their constituency, just like our Skudai candidate, Kartiyaini.
"Kartiyaini is a lawyer who has worked consistently with the party. After careful discussions, we are confident in our choice. We also have 12 Chinese candidates, three Malay candidates and two Indian candidates. This reflects all communities, and that is what DAP stands for," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Authorities probe alleged Quranic verse error in influencer's book
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Authorities are investigating an alleged error involving Quranic verses in a book published by influencer Aisyah Hijanah.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan described the matter as "serious", as Putrajaya moves to strengthen the publishing ecosystem for religious books.
The Malaysian Islamic Development Department (Jakim) has given its opinion on the issue, he said.
"We consider this a serious matter involving negligence in the publication of works containing errors," Zulkifli said.
"This is currently under investigation, and we are waiting for the outcome of the probe," he told reporters.
Zulkifli said religious writers must ensure accuracy in their work.
"While we encourage new religious publications, we must ensure authenticity and validity. This is critical, and we will strengthen monitoring," he said.
He said the government is working to establish the Malaysian Religious Books Publishing Council to bring together publishers and strengthen the sector.
"And, Insya-Allah, we will look into this matter more deeply to determine further action," he added.
Aisyah had earlier issued a public apology on Instagram after her book Wanita Syurga came under criticism.
Despite the apology, Zulkifli said the government remains committed to ensuring such mistakes do not recur.
He was speaking after officiating the 2026 Syariah Lawyer National Convention.
Zulkifli said the Syariah Court fast-track initiative had achieved a disposal rate of over 97 per cent, with most cases resolved within one day.
He said efforts to digitalise Syariah Court services are being strengthened through the e-Syariah Version 3 system.
Its e-filing module allows agencies such as the Legal Aid Department, Syariah Prosecution Department and Syariah legal firms to register civil and criminal cases remotely without attending court counters.
Zulkifli added that the digital Syariah justice ecosystem is being strengthened through the e-Tanzim system, a centralised digital reference hub compiling grounds of judgment and legal references including practice directions, legal principles and scholarly publications.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 1 child or teen drowns every 3 days
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An average of one child or teenager has drowned every three days in Malaysia over the past five years, prompting authorities to call for stronger water safety awareness and closer supervision of children.
The Fire and Rescue Department recorded 619 emergency calls involving child and teenage drowning incidents between 2021 and April this year, highlighting the persistent threat posed by water-related accidents.
Its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said the highest number of cases was recorded in 2023 with 139 incidents, before dropping to 122 in 2024 and 108 last year, Harian Metro reported.
"A further 38 cases were reported in the first quarter of this year, showing these tragedies continue to occur and require serious attention from all parties," he said.
Rivers and irrigation canals were the deadliest locations, accounting for 364 cases, or 58.8 per cent of the total.
Beaches recorded 150 cases, followed by waterfalls and rapids (50), lakes and former mining pools (44), while 11 cases involved children drowning in drains.
Sabah recorded the highest number of cases with 99, followed by Kelantan (95), Sarawak (82) and Johor (78).
Contrary to public perception, Nor Hisham said most drowning incidents did not occur during long school holidays.
Instead, weekdays accounted for the highest number of incidents with 216 cases, or 35 per cent of the total, followed by weekends with 186 cases. School holidays ranked third with 112 cases.
"The risk of drowning is more closely linked to the level of supervision and daily routines than the holiday season alone," he said.
Teenagers aged between 13 and 18 made up the largest group of victims, accounting for 334 cases, or 54 per cent of the total, followed by children aged seven to 12 with 218 cases.
Nor Hisham attributed the high number of teenage drownings to greater risk-taking behaviour, overconfidence and participation in water activities without adult supervision.
He urged parents, guardians and communities to remain vigilant and actively supervise children around water throughout the year, saying early education on water safety was crucial to preventing deaths.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four times a charm? Persistent independent Jeganathan braves Bukit Naning poll again
Article:
MUAR: Three consecutive defeats have not dampened the spirit of independent candidate S. Jeganathan, who is once again trying his luck in Bukit Naning in the July 11 Johor election.
Jeganathan previously contested as an independent in Bukit Naning in the 14th general election (GE14) and the 2022 Johor election, as well as the 2023 Simpang Jeram by-election, but lost on all three occasions.
Despite losing his deposit in those previous bids, Jeganathan said his decision to throw his hat in the ring again was driven by a strong conviction that he could win, alongside requests from voters in the constituency.
"I feel there is hope of winning this time despite losing three consecutive elections. I remain motivated and have never given up on helping people.
"So I decided to contest again, and the people were happy when they found out I was standing," he said.
Asked about the issues he intended to focus on during the campaign, Jeganathan, a contractor, said he would prioritise infrastructure improvements, particularly repairing damaged roads in the Bukit Naning constituency.
"People told me the previous elected representative rarely visited the constituency, and even when they did, it was only certain areas. So residents contacted me to raise the problems they were facing.
"As a contractor, I have my own machinery, including lorries, which I have used to help repair damaged roads," he said.
Jeganathan's political journey began in GE14, when he contested as an independent in a four-cornered fight and secured 364 votes.
In the 2022 Johor election, he contested the seat again and, although defeated, more than quadrupled his vote tally to 1,514.
His third attempt came in the 2023 Simpang Jeram by-election, which was triggered by the death of its assemblyman, Datuk Seri Salahuddin Ayub. He garnered 311 votes and again lost his deposit.
In the Johor election 2026,
Jeganathan faces a five-cornered contest
against Barisan Nasional's (BN) Mohd Ghazali Sabari, PKR's Md Ysahrudin Kusni, Bersama's Iskandar Md Alias and Perikatan Nasional's (PN) Mohd Radzi Amin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Anwar calls on PH candidates to champion civil politics
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today wished all PH candidates contesting the 16th Johor state election a successful campaign.
In a Facebook post, Anwar urged the candidates to engage with the people, not merely to seek votes, but to carry out the trust placed in them wholeheartedly.
The prime minister also stressed the importance of working hard, reaching out to people from all walks of life, and proving that civil politics will always prevail over politics of hatred.
"InsyaAllah, if we are sincere in championing the welfare of the people of Johor, they will make the right choice for the future of their beloved state," he said.
Anwar, who is also PKR president, also quoted verse 159 of Surah Ali 'Imran, which calls on Muslims to place their trust in Allah after making every effort.
Nomination for the 16th Johor state election concluded today, with 172 candidates confirmed to contest the 56 seats.
PH and Barisan Nasional (BN) are fielding 56 candidates each, followed by Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 33, Parti Bersama Malaysia with 15, the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) with four, Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli) and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) with one candidate each, while six candidates are contesting as Independents.
Polling will be held on
July 11
, with early voting on July 7.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt rolls out RM60bil drive to strengthen micro, small and medium enterprises
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government has planned more than 300 development programmes worth RM60 billion this year to help Malaysia's micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) boost growth.
In a statement marking World MSME Day today, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim said the programmes would cover financing, human capital development, market access, infrastructure, as well as technology and innovation.
He said the initiatives are part of the ministry's ABCD framework, which focuses on productivity, simplified bureaucracy, access to capital and market expansion.
"Behind every grocery shop, restaurant, small factory, service provider and family business are millions of Malaysians who depend on micro, small and medium enterprises for their source of income and livelihood," he said.
Sim said micro, small and medium enterprises remain the backbone of Malaysia's economy despite global challenges, including rising operating costs driven by supply chain disruptions and geopolitical uncertainty.
"The sector contributes nearly 40 per cent of Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) and employs more than eight million people, accounting for almost half of the country's workforce."
He said government support for micro, small and medium enterprises had yielded positive results last year, with 344 entrepreneurship and development programmes worth RM31.63 billion helping to create 6,600 new entrepreneurs.
Additionally, the initiatives enabled 50,000 MSMEs to improve their business management, while 8,000 digitalised their operations.
He added that government-assisted micro, small and medium enterprises generated RM870 million in additional sales and RM6.8 billion in export sales.
"Our aspiration is not merely to help micro, small and medium enterprises remain in business, but to enable more Malaysian enterprises to grow into high-value companies, expand beyond domestic markets and become creators of value and innovation," he said.
Sim said a stronger micro, small and medium enterprise sector would create more jobs, strengthen the national economy and improve Malaysians' livelihoods.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH to roll out manifesto focused on federal alignment, development
Article:
KULAI: Pakatan Harapan will soon unveil its Johor election manifesto, focusing on ensuring the state is governed by a coalition aligned with the federal government to secure greater development and benefits.
PH secretariat chief Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the draft manifesto had been finalised but remained subject to final endorsement during a meeting with the coalition's top leadership this Monday.
"It will feature realistic, practical and easily understood pledges for the people, with one of its core pillars highlighting the need for Johor to be administered by a coalition aligned with the federal government to allow the state to enjoy more benefits and development.
"The manifesto this time will feature a framework that is realistic, tangible and reasonable.
"Previously, Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari and I were too idealistic when drafting manifestos, resulting in hundreds of pages covering too many things.
"However, over time, we have become more realistic. A manifesto should be realistic so that the people can understand it and relate to it," he said at a press conference here today.
He said the manifesto would focus on economic stability and prosperity, public welfare, political stability, good governance, increasing state revenue, and fair distribution to the people.
He said PH would emphasise performance-based politics over identity politics, which he described as divisive.
"There are no winners in identity politics. Instead, it only creates tension. That is why we want to offer performance-based politics and solutions to the people," he said.
Undoubtedly, the manifesto will cover all matters, presented in a simpler format without compromising our offerings, he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar to launch LRT3 Shah Alam Line tomorrow
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line, spanning 37.8 kilometres from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, has been fully completed and is ready for passenger service.
Shah Alam Line chief Mohd Ariffin Idris confirmed that the official launch, expected to benefit some two million residents along its corridor, will be officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at a ceremony on Sunday, June 28.
He added that the prime minister will announce the commercial opening date and time for the 20-station route during the event.
"The new stations are Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU 11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato' Menteri and UiTM Shah Alam.
"The remaining 10 stations are Seksyen 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia," he told reporters during a pre-opening media ride on the new service.
Passengers will enjoy seamless transfers, Mohd Ariffin explained, with connections to the Kajang Line at Bandar Utama and to the Kelana Jaya Line at Glenmarie 2.
To make commuting even easier, the network is supported by 40 feeder buses operating across 13 routes with 323 stops, all for RM1 per ride, from 6am to 11.30pm.
"We also offer Rapid On-Demand van services, with 44 vans serving 20 zones at RM2 per trip, available during the same operating hours," he said.
He added that both feeder bus routes and van services will be fine-tuned based on commuter demand and travel patterns once the Shah Alam Line is operational.
For those driving, parking is available at Kayu Ara, Damansara Idaman, Pasar Besar Klang, Sri Andalas, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia stations, with a total of 2,300 bays.
Looking ahead, Mohd Ariffin said that Prasarana Malaysia Berhad (Prasarana) is targeting 67,000 daily passengers in the first year, with that figure expected to rise to 117,708 per day within five years.
The Shah Alam Line is also embracing sustainability, he said, with green features such as inverter-based air conditioning for light rail vehicles and smart cooling systems.
Station designs have been carefully planned to maximise natural airflow and daylight, reducing energy use.
Accessibility is another key priority, with all stations equipped with ramps for wheelchair users, disability-compliant toilets, and reserved seating for persons with disabilities in every carriage.
As for the Tropicana, Raja Muda, Temasya, Bukit Raja and Bandar Botanik stations slated for reconstruction, Mohd Ariffin confirmed that work will begin by the end of this year. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Five-cornered fights set to shake up Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Naning
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Three key Johor state seats are set for five-cornered contests, setting the stage for unpredictable outcomes in Bukit Batu, Puteri Wangsa and Bukit Naning as rival blocs vie for fragmented voter support.
The Election Commission confirmed that all three constituencies will feature five candidates, raising the prospect of vote-splitting in closely watched urban and semi-urban seats where winning margins have traditionally been narrow.
In Bukit Batu, incumbent Pakatan Harapan assemblyman Arthur Chiong Sen Sern will defend his seat against Barisan Nasional's R. Kumaran, Muda's M. Premanand, Bersama's G. Tamili and independent Datuk Kamaruzaman Ali.
In the 2022 state election, Chiong won the seat in a four-cornered contest with a razor-thin majority of 137 votes after polling 9,439 votes.
In Puteri Wangsa, PH's Dr Maszlee Malik will face BN's Teow Chia Ling, Muda's Rashifa Aljunied, Bersama's Nicholas Paul Vincent and independent Wang Wee Siong.
With 112,804 voters, Puteri Wangsa has the second-largest electorate in Johor after Skudai, which has 128,723 registered eligible voters.
The seat, under the Tebrau parliamentary constituency, also witnessed a crowded contest in the 2022 election.
Muda's Amira Aisya won the six-cornered contest with 22,884 votes and a 7,114-vote majority over BN's Ng Yew Aik.
PN's Loh Kah Yong polled 8,957 votes, Parti Bangsa Malaysia's Steven Choong secured 2,471, Pejuang's Khairil Anwar Razali garnered 2,468, while independent Adzrin Adam obtained 398.
The constituency also has a relatively young electorate, with 11,573 voters aged between 18 and 20, while those aged 21 to 29 form the largest age group at 32,776.
Bukit Naning mirrors the same pattern, with BN, PH, PN, Bersama and an independent candidate locked in another five-cornered contest.
Bukit Naning incumbent Datuk Dr Mohd Fuad Tukirin was dropped by BN and replaced with Mohd Ghazali Sabari.
Mohd Ghazali will face PH's Md Ysahrudin Kusni, Bersama's Iskandar Md Alias, PN's Mohd Radzi Amin and independent S Jeganathan.
The five-cornered contests in all three seats are expected to heighten electoral uncertainty, with disciplined grassroots machinery and voter turnout likely to prove more decisive than popularity alone.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 9 arrested in illegal bauxite mining raid, RM3.8mil haul seized in Kuantan
Article:
KUANTAN: Authorities arrested nine people and seized about 10,000 tonnes of bauxite-bearing soil, heavy machinery and lorries worth a combined RM3.75 million in a raid on an illegal mining operation at a Felda plantation in Bukit Goh.
According to Harian Metro, the raid at 11.30am on June 25 under Op Bersepadu Khazanah involved the General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 7 Intelligence Branch and the State Enforcement Unit.
Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure included soil suspected to contain bauxite worth RM2 million, two excavators valued at RM1 million and three lorries worth RM750,000.
Preliminary investigations found that five of the nine suspects were foreign nationals without valid identification documents or work permits.
Checks also revealed the excavation was carried out without the necessary licences and approvals from the local authority and state government.
Investigations showed the land belonged to a Felda settler and had been leased to a company for land-clearing and oil palm replanting works.
"The excavated soil was transported to a processing plant in Gebeng before being sent to buyers," Ahmad Radzi said.
The four Indonesians and one Bangladeshi who were detained were handed over to police for further investigation, while the remaining suspects and seized items were handed over to the State Enforcement Unit, which also ordered an immediate halt to operations at the site.
The case is being investigated under Section 158 of the Pahang State Mineral Enactment 2001 for illegal mineral extraction, as well as Sections 15(1)(c), 39(b) and 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for immigration-related offences
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police tracking individual believed to have been with missing Nor Afiza
Article:
TAIPING: Police are tracking an individual believed to have been with 41-year-old Nor Afiza Zainuddin before her disappearance 11 days ago.
Acting Taiping police chief Superintendent Mohd Ariffridzuan Ezahar said the search for the mother of two was ongoing, with investigators pursuing fresh leads.
"We have not located her yet. We are also tracking a person believed to have been with her before she went missing. We have recorded statements from her family members and close friends," he said when contacted today.
Nor Afiza was reported missing on June 18. She was last seen leaving her mother's house in a white Perodua Axia, reportedly to attend a job interview at the Casuarina Hotel in Kuala Kangsar.
Ariffridzuan said investigations revealed that she did not attend the interview.
Her vehicle was found on June 21 at the Kampung Paya Lintang recreational site in Batu Hampar, near Padang Rengas. Police said the car was in good condition, with the keys and several of her personal belongings still inside.
Nor Afiza is described as 155cm tall, weighing 57kg, with a tanned complexion, a flat nose, and wearing glasses.
Anyone with information is urged to contact their nearest police station, investigating officer Inspector Sanjay Shanmugam at 012-5039820, or the Taiping police headquarters at 05-8291222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: Malaysia must accelerate adoption of drone technology
Article:
SEPANG: Malaysia must accelerate its adoption and development of new technologies, particularly in the Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) or drone industry, to bolster its global economic standing, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the drone industry, which intersects with artificial intelligence (AI) and digital transformation, has emerged as a powerful catalyst for innovation, productivity and economic growth.
"If Malaysia wants to become a more successful country in terms of its economy, it must be fast in developing new technologies and fields.
"Drones are related to digital technology and AI, and we need to build an ecosystem that is suitable and reasonable for them," he said at the MyDrone Expo 2026 (MDX2026) closing ceremony here today.
Co-organised by the World UAV Federation (WUAVF) Malaysia Chapter and the Malaysia UAV Development Association (MUDA), the three-day expo surpassed expectations by featuring more than 100 exhibition booths and attracting thousands of visitors and industry delegates from 46 countries.
Anwar pointed out that the global UAV market is projected to exceed USD55 billion by 2030, while the low-altitude economy is becoming an increasingly important driver of investment.
To seize these opportunities, Anwar has directed Cabinet ministers, policymakers, science research establishments and all related agencies to extend full regulatory and ecosystem support to the industry.
"The policy is clear. Drones and the related industry are given full support, not only for the air force and defence industry but also for civil activities, such as plantations, to enhance productivity," he added.
To further drive the industry, Anwar said the government is committed to improving the regulatory framework, enhancing research funding, testing and certification.
He also urged closer collaboration between private players, government research establishments and universities to build a robust talent pipeline, similar to existing faculties in AI and quantum computing. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election key contests — Machap, Puteri Wangsa, Bukit Kepong in spotlight
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 27 — Several high-profile contests are set to dominate the campaign for the 16th Johor state election, with heavyweight politicians, former ministers, and closely watched constituencies expected to shape the outcome when voters head to the polls on July 11.
Topping the list is Machap, where Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi of Barisan Nasional (BN) faces a straight fight against Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Nur Hafiz Roslan, a lawyer.
The contest is widely seen as a referendum on Onn Hafiz’s leadership after four years at the helm of the Johor state government.
Another key battleground is Puteri Wangsa, where former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik of PH faces the election’s highest-profile five-cornered contest against BN’s Teow Chia Ling, Nicholas Paul Vincent of Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama), Rashifa Al Junied of the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (MUDA), and Independent Wang Wee Siong.
With more than 128,000 registered voters, Puteri Wangsa is among Johor’s largest constituencies and is expected to be one of the fiercest contests in the election.
In the north of the state, Bukit Kepong is shaping up as another key battleground, with former Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal carrying Perikatan Nasional’s (PN) challenge against BN’s Ahmad Syar’e Yusof and PH’s C. Subramani.
Permas will also be closely watched after Amanah fielded Sharon Teo Siew Hui as its first Chinese candidate in a four-cornered contest involving BN’s Baharudin Mohamed Taib, PN’s T. Vela, and Bersama’s Dr Zamil Najwah Arbain.
Attention will also be on Pasir Raja, where former Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba returns to the state political arena after an eight-year absence. Representing BN, he faces PH’s Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim and PN’s Yuhanita Yunan in a three-cornered contest.
Urban voters will be watching Larkin, where BN incumbent Mohd Hairi Mad Shah, who is State Youth and Sports Committee chairman, takes on Pulai MP Suhaizan Kayat of PH, with Parti Bersama Malaysia’s Norsinah Abu adding another dimension to the contest.
Meanwhile, Endau will make electoral history as Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI) makes its election debut. The party has fielded Jati Awang against BN’s Alwiyah Talib, PH’s Saiful Nizam Samat, and PN’s Hasnul Hakimi Hussien in a four-cornered fight.
Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities dean, Associate Prof Dr Mazlan Ali, said several marginal seats narrowly won by BN in the 2022 state election, including Bukit Pasir and Parit Yaani, could ultimately determine whether the coalition retains its two-thirds majority or whether the opposition makes significant gains.
Polling is on July 11, with early voting set for July 7.
For the latest updates on the state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia to review diesel quota rates for commercial vehicles by August
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 27 — The government will review the fixed quota rates of subsidised diesel for several types of land transport vehicles for goods under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) to ensure that the subsidy distribution is more accurate and in line with actual operational needs.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the decision was made following yesterday’s Cabinet Meeting which discussed the status of progress in implementing targeted fuel subsidy through the BUDI Diesel, BUDI95, Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) and Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS) mechanisms.
“Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ordered improvements to be implemented in several aspects to ensure that the people’s feedback is met through strategies and approaches that take into account the people’s cost of living factors, leakage risk management and the country’s position and fiscal space,” he said in a press conference after a field session in conjunction with the implementation of the BUDI Diesel early access, here today.
Armizan said under the improvements, which are expected to be implemented by August, the adjustment of diesel quotas under SKDS will no longer be based solely on average consumption by vehicle type, but will also take into account several technical factors.
Among the factors that will be assessed are the weight with load (BDM), engine capacity, lorry category, fuel consumption levels and the operating pattern of each vehicle.
“There are vehicles of the same type of transport but different BDMs, so if previously there was a uniform fixed quota for the type of transport, which was the same, whereas in terms of usage we know that transport vehicles with larger BDMs in terms of weight, size and also the size of the load, then the fuel consumption would definitely be more.
“So yesterday the Cabinet decided to make an adjustment to the fixed quota not only by looking at the average previous usage, we have the previous usage record, but also by looking at other technical factors so that the quota is more in line with the operating costs of companies involved in the transportation of consumer goods,” he said.
He said the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN), the Ministry of Transport (MOT) and the Ministry of Finance (MOF) had been instructed to take immediate follow-up action to implement the Cabinet’s policy decisions by identifying appropriate channeling mechanisms including the need for modifications and integration into the existing implementation system.
“God willing, KPDN, MOT and MOF will work closely so that the policy decisions made by the Cabinet yesterday are implemented immediately so that more people would benefit from the targeted fuel subsidy agenda,” he said.
In addition, Armizan said the Cabinet also agreed to expand the distribution of diesel subsidies to small traders who have diesel vehicles such as jeeps and pickups registered in the name of a business or company under the class or code of private business use.
He said previously, the eligibility conditions for diesel subsidies were limited to vehicles registered in the name of individuals for BUDI Diesel and business vehicles or goods transport companies under the class or code of use for consumer goods transport for SKDS.
“Yesterday the Cabinet decided to expand this policy because it saw that these were small businesses that we needed to support,” he said.
Meanwhile, Armizan said owners of private diesel vehicles such as jeeps and pickups who were eligible under BUDI Diesel and wanted to apply for an additional 100-litre quota, bringing the total quota to 300 litres compared to the basic quota of 200 litres, could apply online or fill out a physical form.
“There are still those who think that the quota for BUDI diesel is only 200 litres, which is actually not correct, the basic quota is 200 litres for all those who have diesel vehicles registered in the name of individuals, but those who have diesel vehicles such as jeeps and pickups can apply for an additional 100 litres, so it is actually 300 litres.
“Actually, the way to apply is easy, it is on the official BUDI MADANI portal at www.budimadani.gov.my.
